## STEP 3: Add `HostedRenderingIdle()` hook to render targets [DONE]

### File

`REPO-ROOT\Source\GraphicsElement\GuiGraphicsElementInterfaces.h`

### Change

Extend `vl::presentation::elements::IGuiGraphicsRenderTarget` with a new method:

```cpp
class IGuiGraphicsRenderTarget : public Interface
{
public:
  // ...existing methods...

  /// <summary>
  /// Notify the render target that hosted rendering has gone idle, i.e. the hosted controller
  /// determined that no more rendering requests will be issued until something changes.
  /// </summary>
  virtual void HostedRenderingIdle() = 0;
};
```

Add an override declaration in `vl::presentation::elements::GuiGraphicsRenderTarget`:

```cpp
class GuiGraphicsRenderTarget : public Object, public IGuiGraphicsRenderTarget
{
  // ...existing members...
public:
  // ...existing overrides...

  void HostedRenderingIdle() override;
};
```

`REPO-ROOT\Source\GraphicsElement\GuiGraphicsElementInterfaces.cpp`

Provide an empty default implementation:

```cpp

{
}
```

## STEP 4: Trigger `HostedRenderingIdle()` from `GuiHostedController::GlobalTimer` once per idle period [DONE]

`REPO-ROOT\Source\PlatformProviders\Hosted\GuiHostedController.h`

### Change

Add a piece of state:

```cpp
protected:
  // ...existing fields...
  bool idleNotifiedSinceLastRendering = true; // avoid emitting before the first rendering
```

### File

`REPO-ROOT\Source\PlatformProviders\Hosted\GuiHostedController.cpp`

### Change

In `GuiHostedController::GlobalTimer()` replace the early-return block.

Find:

```cpp
if (!wmManager->needRefresh && !windowsUpdatedInLastFrame)
{
  return;
}
```

Replace with:

```cpp
if (!wmManager->needRefresh && !windowsUpdatedInLastFrame)
{
  if (!idleNotifiedSinceLastRendering)
  {
    idleNotifiedSinceLastRendering = true;
    renderTarget->HostedRenderingIdle();
  }
  return;
}
```

In the `NEED_REFRESH:` block, add the reset line:

```cpp
NEED_REFRESH:
wmManager->needRefresh = false;
windowsUpdatedInLastFrame = false;
idleNotifiedSinceLastRendering = false;
```

Ordering constraints:
- Keep the existing `if (renderTarget->IsInHostedRendering()) { return; }` return before any idle notification.

## STEP 7: Unit test framework validates `RendererIdle` sequencing [DONE]

### File

`REPO-ROOT\Source\UnitTestUtilities\GuiUnitTestProtocol_Rendering.h`


### File

`REPO-ROOT\Source\UnitTestUtilities\GuiUnitTestProtocol_Rendering.cpp`

### Change

Implement the handler:

```cpp
void UnitTestRemoteProtocol_Rendering::Impl_RendererIdle()
{
  rendererIdleCount++;
}
```

### File

`REPO-ROOT\Source\UnitTestUtilities\GuiUnitTestProtocol.h`

### Change

In `UnitTestRemoteProtocol::ProcessRemoteEvents()`, inside `if (LogRenderingResult())` and immediately before `frameExecuting = true;`, add validation/reset:

```cpp
if (LogRenderingResult())
{
  // ...existing code...

  if (nextEventIndex == 0)
  {
    CHECK_ERROR(rendererIdleCount <= 1, ERROR_MESSAGE_PREFIX L"Expected at most one RendererIdle before the first frame boundary.");
  }
  else
  {
    CHECK_ERROR(rendererIdleCount == 1, ERROR_MESSAGE_PREFIX L"Expected exactly one RendererIdle between two frame boundaries.");
  }
  rendererIdleCount = 0;

  // Note: do not reset rendererIdleCount when LogRenderingResult() is false;
  // it must accumulate across non-boundary ticks to detect duplicate/out-of-order RendererIdle.

  frameExecuting = true;
  func();
  frameExecuting = false;
  nextEventIndex++;
}
```

```powershell

cd REPO-ROOT\Test\GacUISrc
```

# FIXING ATTEMPTS

## Fixing attempt No.1

- Why it did not work:
  - `copilotExecute.ps1` rejected the request because `-Configuration` was not specified, causing an invalid configuration value (".") and aborting the codegen run.
- What I need to do:
  - Re-run the Metadata_UpdateProtocol execution with an explicit configuration/platform pair (Debug|x64).
- Why this should solve it:
  - The script validates Configuration/Platform inputs; providing valid values allows it to resolve the executable and run the generator.
